methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Astrolight is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Laurynas Mačiulis that has raised €2.8M to date. The valuation landscape for this stage includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, though the immediate peer set at identical stage and valuation tier remains sparse.

Standard triangulation methods encounter material constraints here. Peer-median anchoring is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, fewer than 3 qualify when filtered for stage, adjacent maturity, or similar valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring likewise cannot proceed without disclosed revenue; the 6-ticker public universe trades at a median 59.8x price-to-sales, but this multiple is uninformative absent Astrolight's top-line data. The company's early-stage status and private status preclude conventional DCF modeling absent forward guidance.

Given these constraints, a defensible valuation range cannot be derived from the available quantitative evidence. Any conclusion would rest on post-money multiples relative to seed capital deployed (€2.8M raised), founder pedigree, and qualitative technology assessment—none of which are provided in this dataset. To support a formal valuation, Astrolight would need either disclosed or modeled revenue projections, or sufficient comparable transaction data at the nano-cap seed stage, neither of which currently exists in accessible form.
